medical_history.chief_complaint: Low blood pressure (76/51 mmHg) and bloody gastric tube drainage
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient was in a bed-ridden state for a year due to sequelae of traumatic intracranial hemorrhage. He was on hemodialysis for chronic renal failure. He also had old myocardial infarction and diabetes.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Chronic renal failure, old myocardial infarction, diabetes, sequelae of traumatic intracranial hemorrhage
medical_history.social_history: Bed-ridden state for a year, prolonged use of nasogastric tube

physical_examination.general_examination: Low blood pressure (76/51 mmHg), hemoglobin level was 7.4 g/dL
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Upper GI endoscopy revealed about a 2-cm-wide mass-like lesion with an ulcer and red pigmentation in the upper esophagus, at a distance of 23 cm from the incisors. Actively bleeding and pulsating vessel was seen in the previous esophageal ulcer base.

laboratory_examinations.changes_over_time: Dropped to 5.1 g/dL after two days

radiographic_examinations.findings: Right-side aortic arch with aberrant left subclavian artery (ALSA) taking a retroesophageal course. Proximal portion formed a diverticulum of Kommerell. Esophagus was compressed by the diverticulum of Kommerell. Focal contrast extravasation from the diverticular portion of the ALSA into the esophagus, possibly indicating an arterial-esophageal fistula.
Extravasation from an aneurysmal segment of ALSA

other_tests.findings: First visit: 2-cm-wide mass-like lesion with an ulcer and red pigmentation in the upper esophagus. Active bleeding focus was not found. Second visit: Actively bleeding and pulsating vessel seen in the previous esophageal ulcer base.
other_tests.changes_over_time: From no active bleeding to active bleeding and pulsating vessel

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was confirmed through computed tomography (CT) which revealed a right-side aortic arch with an aberrant left subclavian artery (ALSA) taking a retroesophageal course and forming a diverticulum of Kommerell. Thoracic arteriography confirmed the extravasation from an aneurysmal segment of ALSA. The presence of a nasogastric tube and the patient's symptoms of massive upper gastrointestinal bleeding supported the diagnosis.

treatment_information.specific_treatment: thoracic endovascular aortic repair (TEVAR) and left subclavian artery coiling
